description_long: This is a large, ancient-style ceramic vase with a bulbous body and a narrow, flared neck. The surface is predominantly a pale, off-white or beige color, with visible cracks and signs of age, suggesting it has been restored or repaired. Around the upper shoulder, there is a decorative band featuring a repeating geometric motif in a faded reddish-brown pigment, possibly hand-painted. The neck is ribbed with several horizontal ridges, adding texture and visual interest. The rim is thick and slightly uneven, with minor chips and wear visible along the edge. The overall form is symmetrical, and the vase appears hollow, suitable for holding water, flowers, or other contents.
description: A large, pale ceramic vase with a ribbed neck, geometric reddish-brown band, and visible cracks and repairs. The surface shows age and wear, with a thick, slightly chipped rim.
description_view_0: This view highlights the ribbed neck, thick rim, and a clear section of the geometric band, with visible cracks and repairs on the body.
description_view_1: This angle shows more of the vase's rounded body and the continuity of the decorative band, with fewer visible cracks on this side.
description_view_2: This perspective emphasizes the symmetry of the vase and the alignment of the geometric motifs, with some cracks running vertically.
description_view_3: This view displays the most pronounced cracks and repairs, as well as a slightly chipped rim and the faded geometric pattern.
